observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
regularly maintain reconciled bank reconciliation, subsidiary ledgers and financial statement
balances and immediately prepare the necessary adjusting entries once identified.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the LGU to require the concerned AO to immediately settle or
refund the CAs which are already due for liquidation and send demand letters to officials
and employees, especially those who have long outstanding cash advances as of
December 31, 2024;
b. Direct the designated AOs to strictly comply with the rules and regulations
particularly on the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period to ensure that
all disbursements are recorded in the books in the appropriate time/period;
c. Refrain from granting additional cash advance to AOs with outstanding balance
and instruct the Municipal Accountant to look into the accounts with no supporting details
and those the nature of which are unknown; and
d. Initiate appropriate measures, including the immediate withholding of salaries
and other unpaid claims due for the erring AO who are still unable to settle their accounts
after demand.

We recommended that Management instruct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards for each
commodity or stock and reconcile it with the Stock Cards, record issuances of inventory,
and exert effort to reconcile non- moving balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in
the books to reflect the correct/actual balances of the inventory in the financial statements; and
b. The Inventory Committee to conduct the semestral count and prepare and
submit the RPCI using the required format to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and record the various transactions using the appropriate account as required by IPSAS and
COA Circular 2024-006.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Ensure the regular conduct of a complete physical inventory count of PPE,
reconcile it with property and accounting records, and submit a comprehensive RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to requirements of Section 124 of the
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Sections 6 and 7 of COA Circular
2015-008;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the guidelines stated in COA
Circular 2024-006 and record the issuances of the semi-expendable properties.
c. Instruct the Property Custodian and Municipal Accountant to maintain a
complete, updated and properly accomplished PPELCs and Stock Cards for each
class of PPE, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, useful
life and other necessary information;
d. Prepare and maintain general and subsidiary ledgers and lapsing schedules for
all PPE accounts, indicating therein the beginning balances and its composition and
transactions regularly; and
e. Avail of the one-time cleansing and follow the provisions of COA Circular 2020-
006 to establish PPE balances that are accurate and verifiable.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the updated lapsing schedules
and complete subsidiary/general ledgers with prior years transactions to support the
balances of PPE and accumulated depreciation reported in the financial statements and
to serve as a valid basis for the computation of depreciation expense; and
b. Direct the Accounting Office to locate the supporting documents, reconcile and
validate PPE balances in the previous years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the accounts payable balance and ensure that the correct and accurate balance,
supported with schedules, is presented in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the Due to BIR and to prepare the schedule of the Due to BIR
accounts, aside from the subsidiary ledgers, to monitor the outstanding liabilities, particularly for prior
years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Local Engineer and/or concerned authorized agency personnel
mandated to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects to submit to the
audit team copies of the Results of Test Analyses/Status of Quality Control Test required by
the aforementioned rules and regulations in order to ensure that the completed projects
conforms with agency standards, plans and specifications as part of its quality control
program.
b. Require the Local Engineer/BAC to include Minimum Materials Testing
Equipment in the Technical Component of the Bid, together with the other major equipment
as contained in the Technical Envelope, which shall be subjected to preliminary
examination and further verified during the post-qualification stage.
c. Henceforth, require the Local Accountant not to pass during pre-audit of the
transaction particularly on final payment, disbursement vouchers on infrastructure projects
not supported with Results of Test Analyses from DPWH/BRS/DOST/DTI accredited
testing laboratories for construction materials and concrete which requires testing to avoid
suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Engineer to
implement a sound and effective monitoring system in place to compel contractor’s
compliance of the requirements of Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184 in order to ensure
that the procuring entity will be reimbursed of its expenses in the restoration or
reconstruction works in case of structural defect/failure occurring during the
applicable warranty period. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor
require the contractor of the above-mentioned project to submit warranty security

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
require the concerned contractors to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the
COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the posting of the project signboard to ensure full
transparency and accountability in the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the Due to NGAs account and prepare necessary adjusting entries
related to the above deficiencies.

We recommended that the Management:
a) Formulate documented/written guidelines or uniform policy and procedures in
the engagement of JO and consultant workers to ensure that the hiring process is
systematic and include the standard qualifications based on the work to be assigned;
b) Direct the HRMO together with the department heads/ immediate supervisors
to ensure that job orders and consultants accurately reflect a quantified and accurate
actual work performed and ensure that these are the actual deliverables stated in their
contracts;
c) Conduct regular assessment of the Manpower Complement of the LGU to
determine whether the hiring of a permanent employee is more beneficial than the
periodic hiring of job orders or consultants; and
d) Stop the hiring of JOs/COS that are not within the ambit contemplated under
Section 77 of the LGC which states that the Local Chief Executive may employ
emergency or casual employees or laborers paid on a daily wage or piecework basis and
hired through job orders for local projects authorized by the Sanggunian concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO together
with the other concerned Officials, if any, to submit the complete distribution list and
consumption report immediately to the Team in order to conduct a timely verification
of audit.

We recommended that Management direct the members of the GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) to:
a. Establish a mechanism for periodic monitoring and evaluation of the status
of GAD PPAs throughout the year to promptly identify implementation
bottlenecks and implement all of the programs before the end of the year;
and
b. Submit all the lacking documents and ensure that the Distribution List is
duly filled out.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of paying claims with
improper and incomplete details/supporting papers and effect payments only if the
required supporting papers are attached to the disbursement voucher; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to attach the checklist on Minimum
Supporting Documents to all disbursement vouchers for processing as a helpful tool to
ensure completeness of the required supporting papers and facilitate the submission of
the lacking/deficient supporting documents noted.

We recommended that Management:
1) Direct the cashiers/collecting officers of the slaughterhouse, market fees, and permit fees to remit daily all collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and
2) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to review the remittances made by the collecting officers and implement appropriate actions to stop the practice of accumulating collections before they are remitted by the cashiers/collecting officers.

We recommended that Management require the:
Property Custodian to conduct an inventory count and afterwards prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventory as of December 31, 2021;
Municipal Accountant to timely adjust, reconcile and record inventory issuances in accordance with the perpetual inventory method; and

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
secure the documents of the completed projects and record the cost thereof to the appropriate PPE account;
submit the list of detailed projects recorded under CIP accounts for verification; and
compute and record the necessary depreciation expense of the completed projects.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) submit the schedule and Aging of Accounts Payable; (b) exert effort to collate details relating to the transactions recorded in the accounts payable account; and (c) observe the timely submission of year-end financial statements (FS) and it’s supporting schedules.

We recommended that Management:
adhere to the step by step procurement process as provided by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
ensure consistency of information in the public bidding procurement documentation prepared and submitted.

We recommended that Management:
execute all the projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; and
carefully plan the projects/programs/activities to be included in the annual  20 per cent LDF budget to ensure that they are procurement and implementation ready.

We recommended that Management:
require the contractor to complete the project based on the location specified in the program of works and in the bidding documents; and
Prepare and strictly adhere to the detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs specifications for the infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 2014-009 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 particularly on the utilization of 30 per cent QRF.

We recommended that Management:
Through the BAC, to specify in the BAC resolution the grounds for  resorting to negotiated procurement under emergency cases;
Resort to other alternative mode of procurement aside from negotiated procurement under emergency cases, when applicable; and
Stop the practice of receiving deliveries from suppliers without a valid contract/Purchase Order.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the LGU officials discontinue or refrain from the practice of procuring goods and services through frequent reimbursements and ensure that disbursements are made in compliance with the guidelines set forth in the 2016 revised IRR of RA 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act.  It is likewise recommended that withholding of taxes be made in all procurement transactions of the Municipality regardless of the mode of payment to the suppliers.

We recommended that the Management: (a) instruct the Officer-in-Charge to faithfully accomplish all the details required in the Driver’s Trip Ticket and Report of Fuel Consumption; (b) observe prudence in requisitioning of fuel, oil and lubricants to avoid any excess usage, if any; and (c) ensure that issuance of Delivery Receipt (DR) be made simultaneously with the Requisition and Issue Voucher (RIV) or its Equivalent (Credit Slip).

through Accounting Office to reconcile the contributions and loan amortizations deductions and remittances; and
update the employees record particularly the salary rate reported in the GSIS website to avoid accumulation of premiums that may be computed once the new salary is posted/adjusted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Accountant to review and recheck reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and prepare the necessary adjusting entries and ensure the correctness of entries being processed;
require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Accounting Office copy of the bank validated authorization letters approved by the Municipal Mayor on all intra-agency fund transfers and bank validated notice of ADA fund transfers from national government agencies as basis by the Accounting Office to record the fund transfers; and
establish an effective internal control in the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office pertaining to fund transfers.

We reiterated our previous years recommendations that Management: a) conduct a periodic physical count of supplies or inventory semestrally as required under Section 124 of Manual of NGAS for LGUs; b) require the Property Office and the Accounting Office to maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively for each type of supplies; c.) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for inventories already supported with distribution lists/consumption reports; and d.) require the MSWDO, RHU, MAO, MDRRMO and the Office of the Mayor to submit the SSMI to the Office of the Accountant to serve as the basis for the preparation of JEV and recording of the expenditure.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting and Property Office to: a) investigate and identify the noted differences between the Accounting records and inventory reports that may require adjustment/s; and b) maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards as required under Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS.

We recommended that Management: a) ensure that the delivery periods/construction period in the publicized web are the same in the contract of agreement of the project; b) ensure that the project inspection report of the Municipal Engineer shall be made after the SWA prepared and submitted by the contractor; c) ensure that any change/variation and suspension of work orders shall be approved by the Municipal Mayor; d) ensure that procurement of goods are separately bidded from procurement of infrastructure project/s; e) ensure that in the procurement of goods, specification are properly identified; f) submit the certification from Pag-Asa on the weather condition  on the periods subjected to time suspension for Projects 3, 4 and 5 and approved justification for the work suspension order for Project 4; and g) require the BAC to judiciously review the documents on procurement and ensure that all relevant points tally- dates, amount, periods.

We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Assessor prioritize the updating of its records particularly the Field Appraisal & Assessment Sheet of all real properties in the Municipality so that correct amount of real property taxes may be assessed.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized fund for 2018 to Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledgers as required and revert back to the General Fund the unutilized amount for CY 2013; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to include all the information needed in the MDRRMF Utilization Report.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: a) record the cost of completed projects and procurement of tools/equipment to the appropriate PPE account; b) compute and record the necessary depreciation expense of the completed projects and tool/equipment; and c) record the transfer of completed projects to the beneficiaries to the Subsidy account.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the persons responsible for the preparation and approval of the POW to refund the amount of ₱99,124.81 representing the difference between the corrected ABC and the awarded contract price.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and cause the implementation of the projects as soon as possible in order to achieve the purpose for which it was established.

observations.reason:
The Accounting Office had no basis for recording the fund transfers.

Unrecorded fund transfers were reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2018.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct the physical count of the LGU’s properties; (b) require the Property Office to maintain property records and if possible, require the Property Custodian to attend seminar/s on property custodianship; (c) require the Property Custodian to cancel the AREs issued to employees no longer connected with the LGU and issue  new AREs to employees who are the new custodian of the assets; (d) require the Municipal Accountant to record the ₱514,800, 60 percent PCSO counterpart to the Other Transportation account; and (d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the procurement of materials to proper inventory account and prepare the lapsing schedule as basis of the depreciation of the related PPE.
observations.reason:
Physical count of PPE was conducted, however, the inventory report submitted and the accounting records showed a difference of ₱11,156,679.46.  No PPELC and PC were maintained by the Accounting and Property Office.  The 60% PCSO counterpart on the donated ambulance amounting to ₱514,800 was not yet recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the completed projects in the total amount of ₱11,514,116.92 to appropriate asset accounts and make the necessary adjusting entry on the on-going project amounting to ₱1,700,569.88 by debiting the CIP and crediting the Due to NGAs accounts.
observations.reason:
The amount of ₱11,514,116.92 pertains to seven completed projects. As of December 31, 2018, only one project costing ₱2,183,829.09 was recorded to the appropriate asset account.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

Aside from the current appropriation for  30% allocation for QRF and 70% for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded, it shall also include under a separate  caption, the list of projects and activities charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; b.) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the breakdown/details of the unutilized amount per year unexpended balances’ and disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed under Section 5.1.16; c.) instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the prescribed accounting guideline and entries in recording transactions of the LDRRMF; in Trust Liability-DRRM; and d.) refrain from charging to Calamity Fund those expenditures that does not concern with disaster risk management activities.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Solid Waste Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and all the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation to comply with whatever requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission for the approval of the LGU’s SWMP. observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) observe prudence in the disbursement of government funds; keep track of the charges in the budget to prevent overspending; b.) limit seminar/training participation to those which are necessary for proper administration; c.) limit the number of attendees to the least possible number; d.) require officials who attended the seminar/training to submit written reports and render echo-seminar for the knowledge learned; and e.) require officials who travelled on Official Business to render accomplishment report on the purpose of travel.
observations.managementAction: No similar practice was noted during audit of CY 2017 accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply strictly with the rate of two per cent of the maximum monthly compensation of P5,000.00 for their employer share to the Pag-Ibig Fund, pursuant to Section 1, of Rule VI, Republic Act 9679 and make representation with the PagIbig Fund Management for the application of excess remittances to the succeeding months.
observations.managementAction: Correct premium is being remitted to the Pag-Ibig Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) direct the Secretary to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and Human Resource Management Officer to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of SB Resolutions authorizing the hiring of job orders (JOs) as provided in Section 77 of RA 7160 and Item 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and b.) review the existing pro-forma contract for Job Orders and consider including therein the specific tasks which are expected to be performed by them aside from the office where they shall be deployed.
observations.managementAction: Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolutions for JOWs contract are now supported by department authorizations and contracts.
observations.reason: The assignment of JOWs to different departments rests with the Office of the Mayor. Manpower requirements of each department were not considered in the assignment of JOWs.

observations.managementAction: No prior year’s expenses was charged to current appropriation.
